talk_metadata.talk_title: Asymmetric Emergence of Cardiovascular Disease in Hematologic Malignancy Survivors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have a shocking increase of CVD incidence, especially during the first two years of a hematological malignancy cancer diagnosis"
Speaker admitted: "I haven't looked into biochemical profiles or electrolyte changes yet"

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation of the proteasome inhibitor cardiotoxicity hypothesis
Lack of biochemical/electrolyte data for CVD events

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for prospective biomarker-driven validation of proteasome inhibitor–induced cardiotoxicity in hematologic cancer survivors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we suspect those high risk of developing CVD is treatment induced... proteasome inhibitor treatment group, they have a very high combined score"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics and clinical data to identify patients with high proteasome‑inhibitor exposure and predict CVD risk

unresolved_questions: What molecular mechanisms underlie proteasome‑inhibitor–driven cardiotoxicity?
Are there protective targets identified in the Cell Village assay that can be repurposed clinically?

assets_to_track: Proteasome inhibitors used in multiple myeloma (e.g., bortezomib, carfilzomib)

talk_metadata.talk_title: Decoding Melanoma Immunogenicity: A Comprehensive Proteogenomic, Immunopeptidomic, and Metabolomic Atlas
rhetorical_signals: Speaker noted: "a large percentage of those tumor antigen candidates were not recorded in the existing IEDB database"
Speaker admitted: "we do not see significant differences between the primary tumors versus the metastasis tumors in terms of the DNA alteration pattern"

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of many novel tumor antigens
Limited clinical outcome correlation for the identified ubiquitin subtypes

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting the interferon‑UB subtype with high ubiquitination of DHX9 and AKT1 to overcome poor survival in melanoma
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the interferon UB subgroup has a significant worse survival outcomes compared to the other"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model ubiquitin‑driven signaling networks to prioritize inhibitors that reverse the hyper‑UB phenotype

crispro_opportunity.description: Sex‑specific lysosome‑high subtype (GPUMB fucosylation) as a predictive biomarker for therapy response
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "the lysosome high subtype in male tumors had a significantly worse survival, while in female it was neutral or better"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate glycoproteomics with clinical data to develop sex‑aware predictive models

unresolved_questions: What are the functional roles of the novel tumor antigens not present in IEDB?
Can the interferon‑UB signature be therapeutically modulated?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Endocrine Therapy Adherence and 10‑Year Risk of Cardiometabolic Outcomes Among Women with Hormone Receptor‑Positive Breast Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "longer adherence to endocrine therapy was associated with greater risk of hypertension"
Speaker admitted: "we control for healthcare access at baseline but cannot fully control for follow‑up screening frequency"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Increased cardiometabolic toxicity with prolonged exposure not mitigated by current monitoring
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted increased 10‑year risk of hypertension, dyslipidemia, and diabetes with AI adherence

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Effect of endocrine therapy duration on cardiometabolic outcomes
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Cohort includes patients with varying baseline cardiovascular risk without stratification
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pharmacogenomic markers (e.g., CYP2D6 for tamoxifen) not incorporated
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA or biomarker monitoring of adherence-related metabolic changes
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential confounding by healthcare utilization not fully adjusted

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AI use increases hypertension risk, yet concluded that both AI and tamoxifen should be maintained for survival benefit, ignoring the cardiovascular risk disparity

key_data_gaps: Lack of pharmacogenomic data for tamoxifen metabolism
No prospective interventional trial to test cardioprotective strategies

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify cardioprotective agents (e.g., antihypertensives) that can be co‑prescribed with aromatase inhibitors to mitigate metabolic risk
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted increased hypertension, dyslipidemia, and diabetes with AI adherence
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict drug‑drug interaction networks and prioritize safe combination regimens

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop CYP2D6‑guided tamoxifen dosing to improve adherence and reduce unnecessary AI exposure
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Audience question raised pharmacogenomics as an unaddressed variable
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genotype‑phenotype models to stratify patients for AI vs tamoxifen

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal duration of AI therapy balancing cancer control and cardiometabolic risk?
Can real‑time adherence monitoring reduce cardiovascular events?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Comprehensive Phenotypic Mapping of ALT Regulation Expands the Landscape of Targetable Suppressors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we found that proteasome inhibition leads to accumulation of PML protein and hyper‑ALT phenotype"
Speaker admitted: "we still need to test this in neuroblastoma, glioma, and PANETs"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target accumulation of PML leading to hyper‑ALT‑driven cell death in normal tissues
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted cell death upon PML overexpression, suggesting narrow therapeutic window

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Proteasome inhibition as ALT suppressor strategy
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Screen identified core proteasome subunits (PSMB1, PSMB5) suggesting the approach relies on generic proteasome biology rather than a unique scaffold
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad proteasome inhibition may cause systemic toxicity, limiting clinical index

key_data_gaps: In vivo validation of proteasome‑targeted ALT suppression

crispro_opportunity.description: Repurpose proteasome inhibitors for ALT‑positive cancers by combining with PML‑stabilizing agents to achieve selective hyper‑ALT lethality
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that bortezomib induces hyper‑ALT phenotype and cell death in ALT cell lines
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic lethal networks to identify co‑targets that restrict toxicity to ALT cells

crispro_opportunity.description: Design early‑phase trials enrolling only ALT‑positive tumors with confirmed PML accumulation after proteasome inhibition
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted need to test ALT regulation in neuroblastoma, glioma, PANETs
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can stratify patients based on ALT biomarkers (C‑circles, APBs) for enriched trial cohorts

cited_competitors.name: ATR inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: cited as a clinical approach that tips ALT balance toward hyper‑ALT phenotype

unresolved_questions: What is the therapeutic index of proteasome inhibition in ALT‑positive versus normal cells?
Can selective nuclear‑import blockade of proteasomes achieve ALT suppression with less systemic toxicity?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Protein Complex Remodeling in Metastatic Breast Cancer Cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we identified over 100 protein complexes, almost half of which are novel"
Speaker admitted: "we still need to link dynamic complexes to patient survival outcomes"

key_data_gaps: Lack of functional validation of identified dynamic complexes
No patient‑level correlation with clinical outcomes

crispro_opportunity.description: Target stress‑response assembly (gained after vernalstat) to sensitize TNBC to chemotherapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted a stress response assembly specifically gained in vernalstat‑treated cells
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic drug combinations that disrupt the stress‑response complex

crispro_opportunity.description: Use loss of SWI/SNF complex as a predictive biomarker for chemotherapy resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that the trap‑associated SWI/SNF chromatin regulatory complex is lost in both treatments
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate complex remodeling signatures to stratify patients likely to respond to specific agents

unresolved_questions: Which dynamic complexes are causal drivers of chemoresistance versus bystanders?
Can the identified complexes be pharmacologically targeted?

talk_metadata.talk_title: ASP2998‑TROP2 Targeted Immunomodulatory Antibody Drug Conjugate with Dual Payloads
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the STING agonist component leads to durable tumor immunity and re‑challenge protection"
Speaker admitted: "we have not set a Trop‑2 expression threshold in the first‑in‑human trial"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects in normal epithelial tissues expressing Trop‑2 (cornea, kidney, urethra)
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted cornea, kidney, urethra, and trachea toxicities at high doses

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No quantitative Trop‑2 expression cutoff, risking inclusion of low‑expressing tumors
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized IHC or quantitative assay for surface Trop‑2 density
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential dilution of efficacy signal due to heterogeneous Trop‑2 expression

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Dual‑payload ADC platform (TOP1 inhibitor + STING agonist)
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Core antibody and linker chemistry are similar to existing Trop‑2 ADCs; novelty resides mainly in payload combination
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Co‑formulation of two distinct payloads may complicate CMC and scale‑up
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on high Trop‑2 expression without defined threshold could limit patient selection

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented strong preclinical efficacy of the STING‑containing ADC yet acknowledged that the trial does not yet stratify patients by Trop‑2 level, potentially contradicting the need for target expression

key_data_gaps: Clinical PK/PD of the STING agonist payload in humans
Correlation of Trop‑2 density with response

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a quantitative Trop‑2 expression assay to enrich for responders to ASP2998
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we are not setting a cutoff in the initial trial"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate IHC image analysis with transcriptomics to define expression thresholds predictive of ADC efficacy

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO to model synergistic immune activation from STING agonist and predict optimal dosing schedules
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted durable immunity and re‑challenge protection mediated by the STING payload
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate tumor‑immune dynamics to optimize dual‑payload timing

cited_competitors.name: Existing Trop‑2 ADCs (e.g., sacituzumab govitecan)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as market comparators lacking STING agonist

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal Trop‑2 density required for therapeutic activity?
How does the STING agonist payload affect systemic cytokine release in patients?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting EYA4 Disrupts Genome Stability in Triple‑Negative Breast Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "EYA4 loss induces HR deficiency and sensitizes TNBC cells to DNA‑damaging agents"
Speaker admitted: "we have not yet tested the inhibitor in patient samples"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential compensatory phosphatases may rescue HR function, limiting monotherapy activity
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted dose‑dependent decrease in HR but no in‑vivo efficacy data yet

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Inhibitor identified from screening; scaffold not yet protected by strong IP, exposing to competitor synthesis
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of biomarker‑driven patient selection (e.g., EYA4 overexpression) may dilute signal

key_data_gaps: In vivo efficacy and toxicity of the EYA4 inhibitor
Correlation of EYA4 methylation status with response

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine EYA4 inhibition with PARP inhibitors to exploit induced HR deficiency in TNBC
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker suggested that EYA4 loss creates HRD phenotype, similar to BRCA loss
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic lethal interactions between phosphatase inhibition and PARP blockade

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop methylation‑based assay for EYA4 intron‑exon hypermethylation to select patients for EYA4‑targeted therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported hypermethylation of EYA4 in TNBC correlates with overexpression
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate epigenetic and expression data to define a predictive biomarker panel

cited_competitors.name: PARP inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as a therapeutic class that could be combined with EYA4 inhibition

unresolved_questions: Will EYA4 inhibition sensitize HR‑proficient TNBC to platinum or PARP inhibitors in vivo?
What is the safety profile of systemic phosphatase inhibition?
